title: Jewish settlers in West Bank escalate harassment and terror campaign to annex Palestinian land
subhed: The death of a 14-year-old boy shot dead on Tuesday outside his school in Al-Mughayyir marks a new peak in an unprecedented spiral of violence in the West Bank. Targeted killings, destruction of farmland and expanding settlements have all intensified, largely ignored by political and diplomatic actors.

subhed: Turnkey heat pumps, electric bread ovens and the selection of 100 pioneering territories: The plan aims to be 'broad in sectors and actions.'

title: Meta plans to lay off about 8,000 employees as AI spending soars
subhed: The move comes as co-founder and chief executive Mark Zuckerberg makes a priority of delivering 'superintelligence' in a costly AI race against rivals including Amazon, Google, Microsoft and OpenAI. Reports on Thursday also indicated that Microsoft is looking to trim its ranks with voluntary buyouts of some US employees.

title: Bernard Arnault defuses speculation over his succession at LVMH shareholders meeting
subhed: At 77 years old, the CEO of the luxury group refuses to disclose how he will be replaced.

subhed: Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon reflects on more than three years of conflict in the African country of 50 million people.

title: Boumidjal, the secretive rap producer behind the hits of Damso, Jul and PLK
subhed: Crowned best composer in 2024, the masked artist is nominated for the third consecutive year at Les Flammes, the French rap and R&B music awards. Having produced more than 600 tracks, he is one of the most influential beatmakers today.

title: Louvre showcases 'beautiful unknown' Martin Schongauer, who influenced Michelangelo and Rembrandt
